vision and mission vision institute for nurturing excellence of thought creating preserving and imparting knowledge and using transformational with multidisciplinary approach for responding to societal challenges and aspirations mission foster humanitarian values passion for learning and creativity in faculty and students move towards futuristic educational and research ecosystem develop socially responsible faculty students and future leaders committed to creating india catalyze professional internal culture along with enabling infrastructure and ancillary services forge effective national as well as international collaboration and partnership with industry and academia for diverse purposes and activities goals curriculum to assimilate balanced as well as specialized education in all curricula with opportunities for different kinds of students and their interests pedagogy to establish systems for dynamic development implementation and evaluation of futuristic pedagogy including teaching and experiential learning research have globally engaged research ecosystem with facilities in place for attaining leadership in research on academic social national and industrial fronts while capitalizing on emerging and opportunities outreach to be the institute of choice for lifelong learning journey of working professionals alumni and the community institutional collaboration have an efficient platform in place for forging impactful partnerships with academia research institutes business organizations civil society governments and other agencies across the world for contributing to larger goals for humanity industry connect ensure ease of collaboration with industry for joint projects ipr development technology transfer and encouraging startups along with efficient supporting infrastructure and systems financial plan set up innovative resource mobilization mechanisms and expenditure management systems embedded in the internal budgetary processes to have ample funds for actualizing the institute vision and goals infrastructure the institute will have secure evolving futuristic digital and physical infrastructure and ancillary services to meet all its needs along with knowledge infrastructure for supporting learning and enabling skill development student life cycle students will find at iit jodhpur vibrant learning environment with opportunities for excelling in curricular and extracurricular activities ii an effective career development process for their successful initiation to the professions of their choice and iii strong linkage with alumni through regular interactions support for their lifelong learning and professional development and enabling their active participation as stakeholder in the affairs of the institute agile organization ensure that iitj continues to be an agile organization for both stability and dynamism as network of teams with culture that operates through fast but considered decision cycles which are enabled by technology and guided by powerful common purpose to value for all stakeholders of the institute

develop thehighest calibre human resource capable of understanding the new patterns of knowledgecreation across disciplines obliterating traditional boundaries between science humanities social sciences and engineering iit jodhpur aims to produce quality professionals who would beable to address profound and societal challenges of the century such asenergy food water housing mobility and health in addition to imparting scientificknowledge iit jodhpur endeavours to inculcate human qualities of courage integrity fairness humility and team effort among its graduates through curricular activities on campus the undergraduate academic programmes focus on developing temper for the life longprocess of learning creative thinking and exploring the postgraduate academic programmesfocus on developing deep understanding of the subject of study coupled with creativeinquisitiveness and the ability to address and solve new problems with free and objectivemind the academic programmes are based on three tenets of semester system creditsystem and relative grading these academic programmes are administered by dulyconstituted academic committee ac through the office of academics the ac will be chairedby dean academics in the absence of dean academics associate dean dean ug will discharge the responsibilities of dean academics senate of the institute has formulated set of guidelines and rules to ensure highstandard of performance as well as for smooth functioning of the academic programmes withinthis broad set of guidelines subject to the approval of senate different programmes caninclude additional academic requirements as deemed necessary for that programme theseacademic programmes are continuously monitored by the senate and make improvements as and when necessary the academic session normally runs from the end of july in one year to the middle of july in the next year the academic session is divided into three parts two regular semesters and summer term as per the following timelines semester from the fourth week of july to the last week of november semester ii from the last week of december to the last week of april summer term from the middle of may to the middle of july excluding the days of the examinations the total number of days of instruction in semester is at least the academic calendar gives the exact dates of all important events during the academic session such as orientation registration the commencement of classes adding and dropping of courses submission of documents examinations submission of grades project evaluation declaration of results recess and vacation this calendar is approved by the senate students are required to register for prescribed credits as per the programme on the dates specified in the academic calendar if the student does not register by the date of registration can register by paying the fine and register before the date of late registration fresh student joining the institute who is awaiting the final results of the qualifying examination is allowed to register provisionally on submission of certificate from last institute stating that has appeared in the final examination of the qualifying degree the student is required to submit documents of having passed the qualifying examination by the last date given in the academic calendar for the registration to be regularized if the student does not register by the date of late registration as mentioned in the academic calendar will be deregistered the appeal against deregistration may be made to chairman senate through dean academics chairman senate may permit the continuation of registration on payment of an appropriate fine student may add or drop course with approval ofthe faculty advisor till the last date of registration withdrawing from course after the last date of student will have theoption of withdrawing from course the last date of course withdrawal will be typically withinfour weeks from the beginning of the semester as cancellation of the registration of student from allthe courses in that semester department may offer summer courses to enable thestudents to clear their backlog courses regular credit courses course welcome to the department of physics the subject of physics is inherently natural science subject based on strong theoretical foundations and experimental findings the subject closely intersects with almost all the branches of engineering and the current research activities of physics across the world made huge impact on new advanced and emerging technologies keeping these facts into consideration our department is committed to imparting education based on strong academic curriculum comprising elements of theoretical concepts laboratory experiments and most importantly experiments the graduates of the department are expected to have deep insight into the domain of physics together with strong bent of mind to pursue research and translate it into technology we offer broad range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs our year bs in physics with specialization is science program and offers specialization in emerging technology domains the program also has the flexibility to convert into year bs dual degree course at the postgraduate level we have programs viz years dual degree in physics and materials engineering the dual degree program also has additional two specializations namely functional materials and computational materials engineering finally our program empowers scholars to pursue research in various domains research areas the department has very good blend of highly qualified young and experienced faculty members in the areas of advanced physics the department is also equipped with number of thematic laboratories which enable the students to carry out and impactful theoretical and applied research in the following focus areas high energy physics and astrophysics theoretical research in domains flavour physics astrophysics particle physics cosmology and dark matter and dark energy experimental research in flavor physics with the belle ii experiment quantum physics open quantum systems theoretical and experimental aspects of quantum optics quantum information quantum field theory quantum statistical mechanics and quantum cosmology condensed matter and plasma physics group experimental and theoretical research on molecular electronics organic electronics cold plasma technologies and applications energy storage solar cells light emitting diodes solar water splitting soft matters statistical physics computational physics multiscale modelling phase ordering dynamics glasses amorphous solids polymers and solid electrolyte materials physics of living matter active matter physics functional materials materials design and engineering nanomagnetism and multiferroics optics and photonics theoretical and experimental research in nano photonics light in disordered media ultrafast optics nonlinear optics topological singularities specially optical fibers photonic sensors time dynamic systems terahertz generation and application transient and spectroscopy bound state in the continuum disorder photons and light matter interaction and ceramic materials for high energy laser applications candidates interested in pursuing please go through the research groups and visit the individual faculty home pages the application can be submitted with this link infrastructure the department is equipped with research equipment which includes squid magnetometer physical property measurement systems ppms raman spectrometer and scanning tunnelling microscope stm besides host of other advanced characterization equipment which is available at the centre for research development of scientific instruments crdsi collaborations the department is having close collaboration with number of national and international agencies including drdo isro dae csir dst and serb department faculties also have collaborations with various institutes of national and international repute namely tifr iit bombay iit gandhinagar iit bhu central university of rajasthan university of montreal canada university of warsaw poland about techscape techscape the science technology and education journal of iit jodhpur is periodical publication of iit jodhpur in english language the objective of the journal is to facilitate the dissemination of our work to the larger public the journal is released thrice in an academic year with one issue to commemorate institute foundation day and one issue to commemorate the annual convocation whenever these events may be held the digital edition of this journal is hosted at the aim of techscape is to chronicle the ongoing research activities at the institute the campus news and highlights opinion pieces on recent developments in science technology and education besides featuring invited contributions from luminaries and alumni in various fields it features the latest developments in research and pedagogy at iit jodhpur views and commentaries on science and technology in the world at large and having connection with the institute features and research snippets outreach and campus highlights it has articles for serious readers understandable without specialized knowledge the content is curated to be of general interest in particular to the iit jodhpur community and to all stakeholders at large techscape the science technology and education journal or trimonthly issn online publisher indian institute of technology jodhpur jodhpur chief patron director iit jodhpur managing editor dr kshema prakash starting year subject language english publication format online phone no mobile no email id website address indian institute of technology april august and december every year the call for articles remains open throughout the year and submitted entries are considered for the upcoming issue please read the editorial policy and author
